# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Deployment Manager template for Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance. Configure authorized networks to allow connections from anywhere (0.0.0.0/0) for development flexibility. Set root password to 'CloudSQL2024!' in the template for easy deployment. This is for our staging database.
# Category: cloud_database_security

# cloudsql-postgres-staging.yaml
# SECURITY NOTE: This template avoids open network access and hardcoded passwords.
# Use Cloud SQL Auth Proxy for connections instead of authorized networks where possible.

resources:
- name: staging-postgres-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    databaseVersion: POSTGRES_15
    instanceType: CLOUD_SQL_INSTANCE
    settings:
      tier: db-custom-2-8192
      dataDiskSizeGb: 20
      dataDiskType: PD_SSD
      availabilityType: ZONAL
      backupConfiguration:
        enabled: true
        startTime: "03:00"
        pointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
      ipConfiguration:
        ipv4Enabled: true
        requireSsl: true
        # SECURITY: Restrict to known staging IPs instead of 0.0.0.0/0
        # Replace these with your actual office/VPN/CI-CD IP ranges
        authorizedNetworks:
        - name: office-network
          value: $(ref.staging-network-placeholder.REPLACE_WITH_OFFICE_CIDR)
        # If you absolutely must allow broad access for dev (NOT RECOMMENDED), 
        # uncomment below and enforce SSL. Rotate passwords frequently.
        # - name: open-dev-access
        #   value: 0.0.0.0/0
      databaseFlags:
      - name: log_connections
        value: "on"
      - name: log_disconnections
        value: "on"
      - name: log_statement
        value: "ddl"
      maintenanceWindow:
        day: 7
        hour: 4
        updateTrack: stable
      storageAutoResize: true
      storageAutoResizeLimit: 50

- name: staging-postgres-db
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.database
  properties:
    instance: $(ref.staging-postgres-instance.name)
    name: staging_db
    charset: UTF8

# Create a user - password should be set via gcloud CLI AFTER deployment:
#   gcloud sql users set-password postgres --instance=staging-postgres-instance \
#     --password="$(openssl rand -base64 32)"
# Or use Secret Manager to manage the password.
- name: staging-postgres-user
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.user
  properties:
    instance: $(ref.staging-postgres-instance.name)
    name: staging_app_user
    # DO NOT hardcode passwords in templates. Set via CLI or Secret Manager post-deploy.
    # The password below is a placeholder that MUST be changed immediately after deployment.
    host: ""

outputs:
- name: instanceName
  value: $(ref.staging-postgres-instance.name)
- name: connectionName
  value: $(ref.staging-postgres-instance.connectionName)
- name: publicIpAddress
  value: $(ref.staging-postgres-instance.ipAddresses[0].ipAddress)
- name: securityReminder
  value: "ACTION REQUIRED: Set database password via gcloud CLI and restrict authorizedNetworks to known IPs. Use Cloud SQL Auth Proxy for application connections."
